why_them: Website is built on an outdated BaseKit/ProSite template last modified in 2016 with zero ecommerce functionality — a pet shop with hundreds of product lines is leaving online sales entirely on the table. The shop is physically well-established in Newton Stewart town centre with a loyal customer base but relies entirely on foot traffic. A modern site with an online shop for pet food and accessories would be highly viable given recurring repeat purchases.
recommended_service: Full website redesign with WooCommerce online shop for food/accessories and local delivery options

why_them: The website is a non-functional holding page stating 'under development — coming soon,' with no ecommerce and no mobile optimisation; effectively the shop has no web presence at all. The business is a well-regarded arts and crafts gift shop selling handmade jewellery, ceramics and homeware from 60+ UK makers — exactly the kind of curated inventory that sells online. Active on Facebook but Facebook alone cannot convert tourist browsers into paying customers.
recommended_service: New website with ecommerce catalogue, click & collect or postal sales, and social media integration
source: Direct search, 17reasons.co.uk confirmed broken holding page

why_them: No website found anywhere for this established antiques dealer that has traded for over 23 years — it relies solely on footfall and word of mouth. Kirkcudbright is a tourist honeypot and antiques buyers actively search online before visiting. A clean site with stock photography and an enquiry/booking system would directly capture this pre-visit research traffic.
recommended_service: New brochure/portfolio website with enquiry form, stock highlights and Google Maps integration
source: AntiquesCentral.co.uk listing, no dedicated website found

why_them: Established independent boutique since 1989 with a hotmail.co.uk contact email and a website that, despite using WordPress, has no online shop and presents only basic brand logos. A 35-year-old business with premium designer labels (Italian, Spanish, Canadian) has loyal customers who would buy online in off-season, yet the site converts no digital traffic. The hotmail address also damages credibility with new customers.
recommended_service: WooCommerce online shop with seasonal collections, professional email (@panachefashion.co.uk), and SEO

why_them: Established 1997 with 1,100+ sq ft of stock across rods, tackle and outdoor clothing. The current site uses an outdated Revolution Slider plugin and legacy WordPress with no ecommerce; all sales are in-store only. With the Galloway Forest Park and 7stanes MTB trail heads nearby, the shop could sell permits, tackle and clothing to anglers planning trips well before arrival.
recommended_service: Redesigned website with WooCommerce shop for tackle/clothing, online permit sales, and Google Shopping feed

why_them: The business domain hard-redirects to their Facebook page — they have no real website at all. They stock multiple bike brands (Merida, Raleigh, Dawes, Batribikes), operate an e-bike range and a hire fleet. Located adjacent to two 7stanes mountain bike centres (Glentrool and Kirroughtree), the tourist and cycling market is enormous and entirely uncaptured digitally.
recommended_service: New website with bike/e-bike catalogue, hire booking system, and service booking — built for Google visibility near trail centres
source: TheOutdoorExperts.co.uk, IndieRetail.uk listings, domain confirmed redirects to Facebook

why_them: Independent ladies' casualwear shop open for 30 years, stocking Joules, Seasalt, and White Stuff. The website shows brand logos but has zero ecommerce and says only 'come visit us,' wasting the significant SEO value those brand names carry. A shop of this longevity and brand pedigree in a busy tourist town is underperforming its digital potential markedly.
recommended_service: WooCommerce online shop integrated into existing WordPress site, with brand-filtered product pages and click & collect

why_them: Long-established florist using a Gmail address and a basic builder template — no professional branding or ecommerce order flow. Florist ordering online is now standard consumer expectation and same-day delivery within 25 miles is their stated offering, which is ideal for an online order platform. A branded email address and proper floristry ordering system would immediately increase conversion.
recommended_service: Professional website rebuild with online flower ordering system, branded email, and SEO for Newton Stewart/Galloway area

why_them: Modern GoDaddy-built site with SSL and mobile responsiveness but zero ecommerce — the business mentions 'UK and international delivery available' yet has no online catalogue or cart, just a contact form. Antiques buyers increasingly shop online first; a searchable stock listing with images and enquiry-per-item would directly drive sales.
recommended_service: Stock catalogue with photography, enquiry-per-item form, and optional WooCommerce for lower-value items; replace GoDaddy with a maintainable WordPress build

why_them: No website found — only a Facebook page for this established gift shop selling candles, crystals, greeting cards, photo frames, china, glassware and gifts. The shop is well-reviewed on TripAdvisor as a 'one stop gift shop,' with varied product inventory suited to ecommerce. Dalbeattie is a busy Solway Coast town with tourist trade that this shop is failing to capture online.
recommended_service: New website with online gift shop, local delivery and postal options, and Google Business integration
source: Yell.com, TripAdvisor listings, no website found

why_them: Facebook-only presence for an arts, crafts and gift shop in Langholm that also runs paid craft workshops — a dual revenue stream that is completely unrepresented online. Workshop booking enquiries and gift sales are currently handled via social media only, which severely limits reach and professionalism. At £40/month Nith Digital's entry-level offer is accessible for a small shop with part-time hours.
recommended_service: Simple brochure website with online workshop booking, gift shop gallery, and contact/enquiry form

why_them: The domain gallowaycraftguild.co.uk does not resolve — the website is effectively dead. The guild represents 60+ local craft makers selling jewellery, pottery, needlework, paintings and leatherwork from a Castle Douglas shopfront, with no online sales channel at all. With 60 contributing makers, the cost of a new website could be shared across the membership.
recommended_service: New website with maker profiles, online shop with individual maker attribution, and events/exhibitions calendar

why_them: The website is built on Ueni, a low-cost auto-generated builder; the URL itself (ueniweb.com) rather than a branded domain is a significant trust barrier. The shop sells quality women's clothing from Made in Italy, Lily & Me and Weird Fish — brands with real search demand — but cannot be found organically with the Ueni platform.
recommended_service: Migration from Ueni to a branded domain on Shopify or WordPress with proper SEO, branded email, and online catalogue
source: Direct site visit, no owned domain found

why_them: Community arts charity with a craft shop, cafe and events programme on a Squarespace site that has a basic cart but limited product range and SEO capability. The arts/craft/gift shop and workshop/events calendar are underutilised digital assets. Squarespace limits customisation and costs more monthly than a comparable Nith Digital-managed WordPress site.
recommended_service: Migration from Squarespace to a lower-cost WordPress/WooCommerce setup with an events/class booking plugin and improved product listings

why_them: WordPress site with good design and mobile responsiveness but no online ordering — customers must call to place orders. The business offers same-day delivery across a large area (Dumfries to Annan and Moffat) and describes this well on the site, but the final step of payment capture is missing entirely.
recommended_service: WooCommerce florist ordering plugin with occasion-based product pages and delivery zone checkout

why_them: The domain dalbeattiebooks.com is not resolving — DNS failure — meaning the independent bookshop effectively has no web presence. This is a real community bookshop stocking new, remainder and second-hand books with limited opening hours that make online visibility even more critical. The Hive.co.uk listing suggests they may have previously used an affiliate scheme worth re-establishing.
recommended_service: New website with basic online catalogue, second-hand book browsing section, and Hive/Bookshop.org affiliate integration for new titles
source: Multiple directory listings, dalbeattiebooks.com DNS failure confirmed

why_them: The physical craft/gem shop trades under 'Galloway Gem Shop and Craft Centre' while the online store is branded 'Outback Yarns' — a confusing split identity that damages SEO and customer recognition. Neither name ranks well and tourists searching for the Castle Douglas craft shop cannot find it easily. Consolidating under one brand would be a clear win.
recommended_service: Brand consolidation and website redesign under a single name with unified ecommerce, local SEO, and craft class booking
